The top-level 'lens' field equals 'testability'
No finding has severity 'critical'

notes: 2 findings (p99 baseline method unspecified, window-reset criterion missing HTTP status). Both legitimate micro-observations. without_skill is slightly more restrained than with_skill on this clean ticket, which is appropriate proportionality.
Both configurations achieve 100% pass rate. Testability evaluation is well within the base model's capability — the skill does not unlock detection that the model couldn't do on its own.

Qualitative pattern: with_skill is consistently more granular — one finding per criterion rather than grouping. This produces more actionable output (each finding = one concrete fix) but more findings overall. whether this is better or noisier is a judgment call.

Severity calibration is the clearest differentiator: eval 4 (tautological-ac) has the missing logging AC rated major by with_skill, minor by without_skill. Given that Requirement 4 explicitly mandates logging, major is the more correct severity — this is a real quality advantage of the skill.
Token cost: +2159 tokens per run (~17% overhead) vs completeness-lens's ~13%. The testability SKILL.md is slightly longer.
Time cost: +6.3s per run (44.6 vs 38.3s). More noticeable than completeness-lens's negligible time delta.
The skill adds a consistent additional finding in eval 1: Context's concrete performance target not promoted to AC. This is a genuine gap the base model consistently misses.

Assertions are detection-oriented and pass for both. To measure the granularity and severity-calibration advantages, iteration-2 assertions should test: (a) eval 4 missing logging rated as major not minor; (b) eval 1 performance target finding present.
